COMMENTS: rv32_soc — PicoRV32 + 1KB SRAM + uart_top on sky130A
Clock: 50 MHz (20 ns period)
--- Synthesis ---
SYNTH_STRATEGY 1 = area-first with reasonable timing effort.
The dominant area driver is the behavioral SRAM (8192 DFFs for
256×32-bit storage). Area strategy prevents Yosys from duplicating
the DFF array for timing, which would make routing unmanageable.
SYNTH_MAX_FANOUT 8: the 256:1 SRAM read mux tree has high fanout
at the top of each mux level; 8 limits buffering insertion depth.
SYNTH_NO_FLAT false: keep hierarchy for Yosys — flattening a
3000-line PicoRV32 + 8192-DFF SRAM produces very large netlists
that slow down placement.
--- Floorplan ---
FP_CORE_UTIL 35: lower than the UART IP (50%) to give place-and-
route room for the dense DFF cluster from the behavioral SRAM.
Empirical rule: designs with >5000 DFFs need ≤40% utilisation to
avoid routing congestion failures on sky130 HD.
Die area estimate: ~10,300 std cells × ~4 µm² each / 35% util
≈ 118,000 µm² ≈ 344×344 µm. OpenLane will auto-size to fit.
--- Placement ---
PL_TARGET_DENSITY 0.45: slightly below utilisation to give the
global placer room to spread the DFF array and reduce congestion.
PL_RANDOM_GLB_PLACEMENT false: deterministic placement for
reproducibility — important for tapeout signoff.
--- Routing ---
ROUTING_CORES 4: matches the UART IP config.
GLB_RT_ADJUSTMENT 0.15: reduce global routing resource by 15%
to force the router to use more vias/layers rather than congesting
a single routing layer over the SRAM DFF cluster.
RT_MAX_LAYER met4: stay off met5 for standard cell routing;
met5 is reserved for power straps in PDN.
--- Power ---
FP_PDN_AUTO_ADJUST true: auto-sizes power grid to die area.
FP_PDN_VOFFSET / HOFFSET 12.45: standard sky130 HD values.
--- Timing ---
CLOCK_PERIOD 20 ns (50 MHz). PicoRV32 closes at ~100 MHz on
sky130 in isolation; the SoC target of 50 MHz gives 2× margin
on CPU internals and comfortable slack on the SRAM mux path.
Hold slack is the tighter constraint — the behavioral SRAM has
no registered output, so hold on SRAM→bus→CPU is path-dependent.
If hold violations occur post-route, increase CTS_CLK_BUFFER_LIST
to insert more hold-fixing buffers.
--- Verification ---
RUN_CVC true: circuit validity checking (open/short detection).
RUN_LVS true: layout vs schematic verification.
RUN_DRC true: design rule checking via Magic.
--- Behavioral SRAM note ---
This config synthesises the behavioral reg [31:0] mem[0:255] to
8192 sky130_fd_sc_hd__dfxtp_1 DFFs. For a production tapeout,
replace soc_sram.v with an instantiation of the sky130 1KB SRAM
macro (sky130_sram_1kbyte_1rw1r_32x256_8) and add:
EXTRA_LEFS: path to sky130_sram_1kbyte_1rw1r_32x256_8.lef
EXTRA_GDS:  path to sky130_sram_1kbyte_1rw1r_32x256_8.gds
MACRO_PLACEMENT_CFG: sram_placement.cfg
The macro path also requires one wait-state on SRAM reads in
soc_bus.v (see the annotated macro path comment in that file).
